Title
HUD System Prompt

Purpose
Refactor Shadow Shift's HUD into a cleaner V2 UI system that better communicates health, world state, element state, weapon/combat status, and checkpoint context.

Why This Matters for V2
UI quality affects perceived polish immediately. A better HUD also reduces player confusion when multiple systems are active at once.

Detailed Codex Instructions
Context:
The game already has basic HUD elements and debug/testing controls. V2 should make the interface more structured, readable, and extensible.

Goal:
Build a reusable HUD system with clearer layout, data binding, and state-specific display behavior.

Implementation Steps:
1. Audit current HUD rendering, DOM overlays, and gameplay state display.
2. Separate persistent gameplay HUD from temporary prompts, debug overlays, death/win screens, and contextual interaction prompts.
3. Add clearer display logic for:
   - health and damage feedback
   - current world
   - current element
   - combo/attack info if appropriate
   - shrine/checkpoint prompts
4. Keep the design atmospheric but readable.
5. Structure the UI code so future map/inventory systems slot in cleanly.

Constraints:
- Preserve current HTML/CSS/canvas stack
- Keep the gameplay viewport prioritized
- Avoid cluttering the screen

Implementation Notes
- Separate data formatting from rendering logic.
- The HUD should be readable on desktop and future mobile layouts.
- Use subtle motion/feedback where it helps communication.

Validation Checklist
- HUD is easier to read
- Gameplay state is communicated clearly
- Contextual prompts still work
- The screen remains uncluttered
- The system is easier to extend
